mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of rate-cutting months over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns remain positive when unemployment accelerates beyond the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and existing home sales indicate a synchronized housing demand collapse that transmits to broader consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series decline concurrently, with out-of-sample accuracy below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: An upward inflection in the index of industrial production reflects stabilizing manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production changes coincide with negative 5-day equity returns in >60% of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 212 signal entrenched inflationary pressures that erode real corporate margins and trigger defensive equity positioning, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when CPI exceeds the threshold, with no statistically significant drawdown over the 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month nonfarm payroll employment growth sustains household income streams and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with a rolling Rank IC below 0.02 across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP growth confirms an economic contraction that reduces aggregate demand and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative GDP growth quarters are followed by positive 60-day equity returns in >50% of cases, breaking the historical recession-return correlation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansions in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls signal synchronized supply-side and labor market strength that boosts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive power when either series diverges, with out-of-sample directional accuracy falling below 52% during expansion phases.
